input.tenant_message: I noticed the wiring in my hallway light switch looks really old — the insulation on the wires is crumbly and kind of cloth-like, not the plastic I'm used to seeing. My neighbor told me it might be knob-and-tube wiring. I didn't know this building was that old. Should I be worried about this?

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule a licensed electrician to assess whether knob-and-tube wiring is present and evaluate its condition. Cloth-insulated wiring with degraded insulation is a known fire risk, particularly if it has been covered with insulation or modified over the years. Depending on findings, a full or partial rewire may be required to meet current safety codes.
